title: thymus serpyllum var. serpens
intro: Thymus serpyllum var. serpens, also known as creeping thyme, is a low-growing, spreading herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by tiny, fragrant flowers and tiny leaves. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe and calm the skin, reduce swelling, and prevent infection. The health benefits of creeping thyme include its ability to support respiratory health, relieve stress and anxiety, and provide antioxidant protection against oxidative stress and cell damage. Creeping thyme can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or topical oil, and is often added to skincare products, perfumes, and culinary dishes for its distinctive flavor and aroma.
